name: Antares

overview.value: Antares known during early development as Taurus II, is an expendable launch system developed by Orbital Sciences Corporation (now part of Northrop Grumman Innovation Systems after Northrop Grumman acquired Orbital ATK) and the Yuzhnoye Design Bureau to launch the Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station as part of NASA's COTS and CRS programs. Able to launch payloads heavier than 5,000 kg (11,000 lb) into low-Earth orbit, Antares is the largest rocket operated by Orbital ATK. Antares launches from the Mid-Atlantic Regional Spaceport and made its inaugural flight on April 21, 2013.

notes: Family-level profile. Flight counts script-aggregated across Launch Library variant configurations (Antares 110, Antares 120, Antares 130, Antares 230, Antares 230+, Antares 330) within the single cited query response; status reflects all variants marked inactive in Launch Library. Capability figures left null, variant-dependent. Last flight date pending the weekly registry sweep.
